domain: Built-in code-writing tools the agent uses directly (file/edit/bash/build-validation), not via sub-agents
summary: The direct code-writing surface is real and fairly mature. `@elizaos/plugin-coding-tools` exposes FILE (read/write/edit/grep/glob/ls), SHELL (run via /bin/bash -c with cloud/local-safe/local-yolo/capability-router dispatch + history), and WORKTREE (enter/exit) actions, gated to code/terminal/automation contexts with role gates, backed by a SandboxService path blocklist, FileStateService read-before-write mtime gating, RipgrepService, and SessionCwdService. Build/quality validation is NOT in coding-tools — it lives in `@elizaos/plugin-app-control`'s AppVerificationService (typecheck/lint/test/build/launch/browser-screenshot/structured-proof) which the APP create/edit flow wires into the coding sub-agent via a validator contract. Unit + deterministic-scenario coverage is strong (4000+ test LOC in coding-tools, real-tsc integration test for verification), but the end-to-end create→coding-agent→verify→screenshot loop is never exercised with a live model (scenarios deliberately omit START_CODING_TASK), and there are zero committed evidence artifacts (no trajectories, video, screenshots, or timelines) for this domain. The stale NFT-drop plugin scope entry has since been removed from this domain.

existing.whatItDoes: Registers the three direct coding actions plus SandboxService, FileStateService, SessionCwdService, RipgrepService. Auto-enables on features.codingTools and terminal-capable env (off on store/iOS, Android only in local-yolo).

action=run executes via /bin/bash -c with timeout clamp [100,600000], cwd validation, history view/clear, and heavy NL-driven command rewriting (disk/memory/health/source-search/crypto endpoint canonicalization). Surfaces exit code, cwd, took, timeout, signal, sandbox backend; truncates streams at 30k chars.

String-replace edit with sandbox path validation, assertWritable stale-read gate, ambiguity check (multi-match requires replace_all), no_match failure, detectSecrets guard before write, recordWrite after. Returns replacement count + first line.
Validates path, gates on assertWritable, detectSecrets guard, mkdir -p + writeFile, recordWrite. Non-atomic (direct writeFile, no temp+rename).

Dispatches by runtime mode: capability-router PTY first, then cloud (throws), local-safe (SandboxManager.exec, refuses outside workspace), local-yolo (host /bin/bash -c). Handles bash/zsh startup flags, process-group kill on timeout (SIGTERM then SIGKILL), zsh->bash fallback, missing-tool detection.
Blocks user-private + per-OS system paths by default; optional allow-roots via CODING_TOOLS_WORKSPACE_ROOTS; realpath-resolves and rejects traversal. Validated before every fs access.

Structured verification pipeline: runs package scripts typecheck/lint/test/build, app launch poll, headless puppeteer browser check with screenshot + vision description, and a structured-proof check that cross-validates the agent's claimed APP_CREATE_DONE/PLUGIN_CREATE_DONE counts against actual test output. Persists per-check .log files under <stateDir>/app-verifications/<runId>/, returns verdict + retryablePromptForChild.

parseTscOutput/parseEslintOutput/parseVitestOutput diagnostic parsers, detectPackageManager, ensureVerificationDir, captureScreenshotViaDevApi (/api/dev/cursor-screenshot), describeScreenshotWithVision (IMAGE_DESCRIPTION model).

Multi-turn scaffold flow: fuzzy-match installed apps, render [CHOICE] block, copy min-project template with token rewrite, then dispatch a coding sub-agent via START_CODING_TASK with the app-verification validator (onVerificationFail=retry) using spawnWithTrajectoryLink. Errors surface as failure ActionResults with reason text.
TaskExecutor that maps coding-verb task specs to START_CODING_TASK/CREATE_TASK action, rewrites output in character voice. Bridges direct tools to the orchestrator contract.

Lower-level sandboxed shell: SHELL_ALLOWED_DIRECTORY restriction, PTY, background named sessions (poll/send-keys/kill), per-conversation history, ExecApprovalService. The coding-tools SHELL action consumes its history service.
Spawns one worker_threads Worker per isolation=worker app with typed RPC invoke(); auto-starts persisted worker apps on boot. Relevant to modify/reload of generated apps.
Mobile-safe read/write/list bridge (Capacitor on iOS/Android, node:fs on desktop/AOSP) that coding-tools FILE target=device delegates to. normalizeDevicePath rejects absolute/.. /NUL.

Capacitor plugin hosting the agent runtime on-device (iOS xcframework/JSCore, Android foreground service). Infrastructure that enables the agent (and thus its coding tools) to run on mobile; not itself a code-writing tool.
App discovery/launch/run-state library + /api/apps/* routes. Supports the launch step of verification; not a code-writing tool itself.

tests.covers: 43 tests: capability-router preference, foreground exec, empty-stream marking, blocklist cwd rejection, timeout + orphaned-child kill, cwd grounding/rewrite logic, URL quoting, all NL command-canonicalization rewrites, user-facing-text projection, command_failed paths, history view/clear.
tests.gaps: All via injected fakes/mocks; no real long-running build (bun run build) validation, no concurrent shell sessions, no real PTY/background session through coding-tools, no cloud-mode refusal asserted end-to-end.

gaps.title: No live-LLM end-to-end create-app loop (scaffold -> coding agent -> verify -> screenshot)
gaps.description: The full direct-coding pipeline is never proven with a real model. plugin-app-control/test/scenarios/app-create-no-existing.scenario.ts is live-only but deliberately omits START_CODING_TASK and accepts a 'could not dispatch' result as pass, so no real app is ever generated, verified, or screenshotted. deterministic-coding-tools-actions.scenario.ts uses strict pre-registered fixtures, so the model never selects a tool. There is zero real-LLM trajectory evidence for direct code writing.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a live-only scenario under plugins/plugin-app-control/test/scenarios that registers START_CODING_TASK + AppVerificationService and runs APP create against a live model end-to-end, asserting: template copied, child coding agent edited files, AppVerificationService verdict=pass, screenshot.png + per-check logs produced. Run via eliza-scenarios run with --report and --run-dir to capture a real JSONL trajectory + run viewer, and commit the artifacts to .github/issue-evidence/.

gaps.title: build / launch / browser-screenshot verification checks lack integration coverage
gaps.description: AppVerificationService.runBuild, runLaunchCheck, and runBrowserCheck (real puppeteer + screenshot + vision) are only unit-mocked. The integration test (app-verification.integration.test.ts) covers typecheck/lint/test/structured-proof with real tsc but stops there. The browser path that produces the only real screenshot evidence is never exercised against a real launched app + real chromium in CI.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add an integration test that scaffolds a tiny real app, runs profile=full with a launched dev server (or a static fixture server) and ELIZA_CHROME_PATH set, and asserts a non-empty screenshot.png is written and the browser check passes/fails on injected console errors. Gate on chromium availability with itIf, similar to the existing bun/npm gate.

gaps.title: No committed evidence artifacts (trajectory/screenshot/video/timeline) for the coding domain
gaps.description: Per the repo Definition of Done, agent/action/tool changes require real-LLM trajectories, before/after screenshots, and a video walkthrough. .github/issue-evidence/ contains only cloud UI PNGs; nothing for FILE/SHELL/WORKTREE or app-create. No .webm/.mp4 and no test:e2e:record output exists for this domain.
gaps.suggestedWork: Create a recorded walkthrough harness: drive a real agent through 'create an app that does X', capture the chat UI + generated files + verification verdict via bun run test:e2e:record (video) plus the scenario-runner JSONL trajectory and the AppVerificationService screenshot/logs. Land them under .github/issue-evidence/<issue#>-coding-create-app/.

gaps.title: No concurrency / many-projects coverage
gaps.description: Phase 3 goal of 10 concurrent coding projects has no test. AppWorkerHostService spawns workers but is unit-tested per-app; there is no test of multiple simultaneous app-create dispatches, multiple verification runs writing to distinct runIds, or SessionCwdService/SandboxService isolation across many concurrent conversations.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a stress/scenario test that fires N (>=10) concurrent APP create dispatches (or N concurrent SHELL/FILE sessions in distinct rooms) and asserts: per-conversation cwd/sandbox isolation holds, verification runIds do not collide, and no cross-room file leakage. Capture a timeline of start/finish per run.

gaps.title: No rollback / undo for direct code edits
gaps.description: On verification failure the flow only retries with retryablePromptForChild; there is no git-revert/restore-backup path and no test for rolling back a bad generated app or a failed multi-file edit. FileStateService tracks mtime for stale-read protection but does not snapshot prior contents.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a backup/rollback capability (e.g. snapshot edited files or use a worktree/branch per task with git reset on failure) and a scenario asserting that a failed verification leaves the workdir restorable. At minimum, test the WORKTREE exit cleanup path as the rollback boundary for create flows.

gaps.title: SHELL action has no real build-failure -> diagnostic surfacing test
gaps.description: bash.test.ts asserts command_failed on non-zero exit, but there is no test where the agent runs a real build/typecheck via SHELL, the build fails, and the failure (exit code + truncated stderr) is surfaced to the planner in a way a follow-up action can act on. Build validation only happens in app-control's separate verification service, not via the direct SHELL tool the agent uses for ad-hoc builds.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a deterministic scenario where SHELL runs a failing 'bun run build'/'tsc' in a seeded project and assert the ActionResult carries exit_code + stderr tail, and that a subsequent FILE edit + re-run SHELL produces exit 0. Capture the two-step trajectory.

gaps.title: Mobile/device backends (Capacitor iOS/Android) untested for file writes
gaps.description: DeviceFilesystemBridge only has Node-backend round-trip tests; the Capacitor iOS/Android path (FILE target=device) is never exercised. native-bun-runtime native engine paths are only tested via web-fallback stubs. Platform coverage (AOSP/iOS/Play) for the file-write tool is effectively unproven.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a device-backed integration harness (or at minimum a contract test with a mocked Capacitor Filesystem) that exercises read/write/list through the Capacitor backend, and document the manual on-device evidence step (screenshot + log) for AOSP/iOS in PR_EVIDENCE flow.

domain: Sub-agent orchestration via ACP (plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator, plugin-sub-agent-claude-code, packages/sweagent)
summary: plugin-agent-orchestrator is the canonical, mature ACP orchestration plugin: it spawns local coding agents (elizaos, pi-agent, opencode, codex, claude) as subprocesses via either an embedded native ACP JSON-RPC client (NativeAcpClient in acp-native-transport.ts, default) or a legacy acpx CLI wrapper, routes their terminal events back into the runtime as synthetic memories (SubAgentRouter), and layers durable tasks (OrchestratorTaskService), a structured provision→turn→submit smithers runner, workspace/git/GitHub lifecycle, multi-account credential injection, trajectory linkage, and an interruption decider. Unit coverage is genuinely deep and green (acp-service 73 cases, acp-native-transport 21, sub-agent-router large, multi-account 6+, interruption-decider 9, plus ~60 unit files). The agent-spawning/routing/credential logic is solid-to-partial. However the LIVE end-to-end evidence layer is broken and stale: the offline composed multi-account E2E works, but the headline live smoke (task-agent-live-smoke.ts) is referenced by the e2e test at a non-existent path (packages/app-core/test/scripts vs the real packages/core/test/live), imports a PTYService the plugin does not export, only supports framework claude/codex and modes sequential/web (the e2e calls a counter-app mode that the smoke rejects), and verifies served apps via raw net/HTTP reachability + sentinel strings rather than screenshots or video. There are no saved trajectory/screenshot/video/timeline artifacts for this domain anywhere in the repo, and no scenario-runner scenario exercises the orchestrator/sub-agent path. plugin-sub-agent-claude-code is a separate, smaller sandboxed claude-CLI driver (not ACP), and packages/sweagent is security-patched vendor source not wired into runtime.

existing.component: AcpService (subprocess lifecycle, transport selection, spawn/send/cancel/stop, session reconcile + acpx-orphan GC, baseline-sha capture, account selection)
existing.whatItDoes: 2941-line core: spawnSession() normalizes agentType, resolves workdir, selects a coding account + injects credentials, then branches to native (spawnNativeSession + NativeAcpClient) or cli (runAcpx) transport; nativeAgentCommand() resolves per-agent commands (ELIZA_<AGENT>_ACP_COMMAND overrides, codex/claude default to pinned npx, opencode prefers vendored shim); sendPrompt/cancel/stop/getChangedPaths; emits typed SessionEvents.

927-line ACP client: initialize/session.new/session.prompt/cancel/close, handles agent->client requests (session/update, request_permission with approvalPreset gating, fs read/write scoped to cwd via realpath + symlink rejection, terminal create/output/wait/kill/release with process-group signaling), parses ELIZA_ACP_MCP_SERVERS for MCP parity, 300s default timeout.

2606-line service: subscribes to AcpService.onSessionEvent, posts task_complete/error/blocked as synthetic inbound memories to runtime.messageService.handleMessage with origin routing keys (roomId/userId/messageId), per-session round-trip cap (default 32) with lineage reset on task_complete, cross-session completion dedupe, changeset/verified-URL gating, dead-URL suppression.

existing.component: OrchestratorTaskService (durable task store + ACP event bridge)
existing.whatItDoes: 2115-line durable task lifecycle: spawns via acp.spawnSession (L1710), bridges ACP events to task records, drives follow-ups (sendToSession), stop/archive; backs the /api/orchestrator routes.

normalizeTaskAgentAdapter maps aliases to elizaos/pi-agent/claude/codex/opencode; resolvePinnedAdapter (fixed strategy reads ELIZA_ACP_DEFAULT_AGENT/BENCHMARK_TASK_AGENT); resolveSpawnWorkdir via TASK_AGENT_WORKDIR_ROUTES JSON rules + convention auto-detect under TASK_AGENT_WORKDIR_ROOTS.

existing.component: smithers-task-runner / executor / integration (default structured execution path)
existing.whatItDoes: Default-on (ELIZA_ORCHESTRATOR_SMITHERS!=0) provision->turn(per-agent, parallel fan-out)->approval->submit workflow graph; each step delegates to ACP turns; executor + integration + db-backend covered by unit tests.

Pure decideInterruption() gives coding sub-agents a shouldRespond-equivalent: deliver/queue/interrupt/ignore based on stop/redirect/additive patterns, addressing, multiParty, sessionBusy; SubAgentInbox queues QUEUE/INTERRUPT text and drains on idle.

selectCodingAccount picks least-used pooled account per agent type; injects per-account creds (claude->CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, codex->per-account CODEX_HOME/auth.json, opencode->pooled key) and drops parent ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY; verified by multi-account-spawn unit test + compose-multi-account-e2e.

withLinkedSpawn wraps spawnWithTrajectoryLink from core: stamps parentTrajectoryStepId into child metadata + ELIZA_PARENT_TRAJECTORY_STEP_ID env and calls handle.linkChild(childId). Wires the data model for cross-agent trajectories; unit-tested. No evidence it is actually exercised end-to-end with a real captured trajectory.

existing.component: plugin-sub-agent-claude-code (sandboxed claude CLI driver, NOT ACP)
existing.whatItDoes: Separate plugin: wraps the claude CLI in an OS-sandboxed (sandbox-exec/bwrap) Bun subprocess with env allowlist/blocklist, cwd realpath validation, binary allowlisting, redacted session transcripts + audit hash. Has unit tests (sandbox, sub-agent-service, session-recorder, plugin). Not part of the ACP transport path.

Security-remediated copies of SWE-agent python/typescript inspector + tools (path-traversal/SSRF/command-injection fixes). Explicitly NOT a runtime-imported plugin; only safe-path/safe-url unit tests. Not wired to the ACP orchestrator.

Python benchmark orchestrator that runs elizaos/opencode (and other) adapters across a matrix, normalizes results into SQLite/JSON, writes per-cell trajectories/ dirs, and serves a browser viewer; supports trajectory-review enforcement. A real trajectory-evidence system but a separate benchmark harness, not wired to the ACP plugin live path.

tests.path: plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/__tests__/unit/ (60+ files: acp-service.test.ts 73, acp-native-transport.test.ts 21, sub-agent-router.test.ts, multi-account-spawn.test.ts 6, spawn-agent.test.ts 13, send-to-agent.test.ts, create-task.test.ts, session-store.test.ts 12, interruption-decider.test.ts 9, smithers-*.test.ts, coding-account-selection.test.ts, sub-agent-identity.test.ts, claude-oauth-token.test.ts, etc.)
tests.covers: Spawn argument/command resolution, transport selection, ACP JSON-RPC framing + permission/fs/terminal handlers, synthetic-memory routing keys + round-trip cap, multi-account credential injection + parent-key drop + least-used rotation, session persistence tiers, interruption decisions, smithers graph, route handlers, SSRF guard. gaps.title: Live task-agent E2E harness is broken on three independent axes
gaps.description: task-agent-live.e2e.test.ts resolves the smoke at packages/app-core/test/scripts/task-agent-live-smoke.ts (does not exist; real file is packages/core/test/live/task-agent-live-smoke.ts), the smoke imports PTYService which the plugin no longer exports (PTYService exists nowhere in source), and the smoke only accepts modes sequential|web while the e2e calls --mode counter-app for 3 of its 6 tests (the smoke prints Usage and exits non-zero). Net result: the flagship 'create a counter app / serve a webpage / keep a session alive' live coverage cannot run even with valid credentials.
gaps.suggestedWork: Repoint the e2e test path to packages/core/test/live/task-agent-live-smoke.ts (or move the smoke under app-core), replace the PTYService import with AcpService + tasksAction (the current exported surface), and add a counter-app mode to the smoke (or drop those e2e cases). Add a CI/local check that imports the smoke module to catch import-shape drift.

domain: Smithers workflow engine — in-process workflow execution (plugins/plugin-workflow) and durable coding-task orchestration (plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator smithers-task-*)
summary: There are two distinct Smithers integrations. (1) plugin-workflow's in-process engine (smithers-runtime.ts + embedded-workflow-service.ts) spawns a fresh Bun subprocess per run, compiles an n8n-style node DAG into native Smithers control flow (dependency-depth levels → parallel groups joined in sequence), delegates each node's execution back to the parent over a line-delimited stdin/stdout protocol, honors n8n retry/continueOnFail, reports per-run engine metrics, and persists durable state to sqlite/postgres/pglite. It is fed by a RAG generation pipeline (keywords → catalog search → match → feasibility → LLM generate → validateAndRepair → fixWorkflowErrors). (2) plugin-agent-orchestrator's durable coding-task path (smithers-task-runner.ts) models a coding task as provision → durable per-agent turn loop (parallel fan-out) → approval gate → submit, crash-resumable via a stable runId + force:false; SmithersTaskExecutor drives ACP coding-agent turns. Both engines are genuinely mature in code and have real (subprocess-spawning) unit/integration tests that pass green. The critical gap: the orchestrator's rich multi-step graph (provision/submit/approval/parallel/multi-turn) is NOT wired into production — runCreate calls runDurableTask single-turn (maxTurns:1, no provision/submit/approval/parallel), so it is only a behavior-preserving durable wrapper around a single prompt. There are also no real-LLM trajectory / screenshot / video / timeline evidence artifacts and no scenario-runner scenarios for either Smithers path; RAG generation is tested only with mocked useModel.

existing.component: In-process Smithers workflow engine (DAG→parallel/sequence, stdin/stdout node-execution protocol, n8n retry/continueOnFail, per-run metrics)
existing.whatItDoes: runWorkflowWithSmithers spawns a per-run Bun subprocess running smithers-orchestrator; createSmithersScript builds the node graph with computeLevels (dependency-depth → parallel groups joined by workflow.sequence), delegates each node back to the parent via newline-delimited JSON requests, honors retryOnFail/maxTries/waitBetweenTries/continueOnFail, emits engine metrics (nodes/levels/maxConcurrency/started/finished/failed/skipped/retries). resolveSmithersDbConfig selects sqlite/postgres/pglite with feature-detect fallback.

existing.component: EmbeddedWorkflowService production caller of the in-process engine
existing.whatItDoes: resolveExecutionPlan (lines ~2360) topologically orders enabled nodes + builds incoming-connection map into a SmithersExecutionPlan; runWorkflow (lines ~2423) persists a pending execution then calls runWorkflowWithSmithers with runNode=executeNode, saves the completed execution, and records engine metrics. This is the real in-process execution runtime (no HTTP sidecar).

extractKeywords → matchWorkflow → assessFeasibility → generateWorkflow → fixWorkflowErrors; structured-output LLM calls (schemas in src/schemas) with provider routing (Cerebras→openai mapping). WorkflowService.generateWorkflowDraft (workflow-service.ts ~335) chains keywords→node search→generation→validateAndRepair→positioning with up to 3 repair passes.

existing.component: Durable Smithers coding-task runner (provision→turn-loop→approval→submit, parallel fan-out, crash-resume)
existing.whatItDoes: runTaskWithSmithers spawns a per-task Bun + smithers-orchestrator subprocess; createTaskScript builds provision step + per-agent wf.loop (until done / maxIterations onMaxReached return-last) optionally wf.parallel across agents + approval + submit; delegates each step to the host TaskStepExecutor over stdin/stdout; assembles TaskRunResult (status completed/incomplete/denied, turns, agentsDone, workspace, submit, metrics). force:false + stable runId gives crash-resume. resolveSmithersDbConfig mirrors the workflow engine.

Implements TaskStepExecutor against a minimal AcpLike (spawnSession/sendPrompt/findResumableSessionByLabel); lazily spawns or reattaches a session by label for durable resume, sends initial vs continue prompt per turn, detectTurnDone classifies stopReason (max/length/interrupt → not done), records lastResponse/lastError, pluggable provision/approval/submit callbacks.

existing.component: Smithers task integration seam + production wiring
existing.whatItDoes: shouldUseSmithersTaskRunner (default on, ELIZA_ORCHESTRATOR_SMITHERS=0 to disable); acpServiceToAcpLike adapts AcpService; runDurableTask drives an already-spawned session through runTaskWithSmithers at maxTurns:1 (single-turn drop-in) and rethrows executor.lastError. Wired in actions/tasks.ts runCreate→runPromptViaSmithers (line ~591/410).

Defines TaskRunSpec (taskId/runId/provision/submit/approvalBeforeSubmit/maxTurns/parallelAgents), TaskStepContext, TaskStepExecutor seam, TaskRunResult/TaskRunMetrics. Documents the full provision→loop→approval→submit→summarize model.

existing.component: Trajectory link service for spawned sub-agents

This is the trajectory-capture plumbing for the orchestration path (not Smithers-specific).

gaps.title: Orchestrator Smithers graph capability is built + unit-tested but NOT wired into production
gaps.description: runTaskWithSmithers supports provision→multi-turn-loop→approval→submit with parallel agent fan-out, and the unit tests prove all of it works against the real Smithers subprocess. But the only production caller, runDurableTask (smithers-task-integration.ts) hardcodes maxTurns:1 with no provision/submit/approval/parallelAgents, and runCreate (actions/tasks.ts ~591) just uses it as a durable wrapper around a single prompt. Workspace provision/submit are separate TASKS sub-actions, not driven through the Smithers graph. So 'smithers workflows drive multi-step coding tasks' is true of the engine but false of the shipped product path.
gaps.suggestedWork: Either (a) wire a real multi-step config through to runDurableTask (pass maxTurns from the round-trip cap, provision:true when a workspace task, submit/approvalBeforeSubmit when PR submission is requested, parallelAgents for swarm tasks) and add a wiring test in actions/tasks.ts that asserts the spec passed to runTaskWithSmithers; or (b) explicitly document that the production path is intentionally single-turn and the graph is future capability. Add an integration test that runs the full provision→turns→approval→submit graph against the FakeAcp via the production runCreate path.

domain: Multi-account quota tracking + switching (codexbar-style) — the AccountPool bridge: per-provider account selection by strategy (priority/round-robin/least-used/quota-aware), live session/weekly usage % probing for Claude + Codex, per-account credential injection on coding-agent spawn, usage attribution, and health-based switching.
summary: A real, well-architected multi-account pool exists and is wired end-to-end for Claude (anthropic-subscription) and Codex (openai-codex), plus opencode→cerebras-api. The brain is AccountPool (packages/app-core/src/services/account-pool.ts) with four strategies; usage % is genuinely fetched live from provider endpoints via pollAnthropicUsage/pollCodexUsage (packages/app-core/src/services/account-usage.ts) and stored as LinkedAccountUsage.sessionPct/weeklyPct. The orchestrator plugin reads the shared CODING_AGENT_SELECTOR_BRIDGE_SYMBOL bridge installed by coding-account-bridge.ts; on spawn AcpService.spawnSession selects an account, injects per-account env (CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN / per-account CODEX_HOME+auth.json / CEREBRAS_API_KEY), drops parent keys, and stamps non-secret account meta on the session. Switching is driven by health flags (markRateLimited/markNeedsReauth on 401/403/429 and login_required) + filterEligible exclusion, plus burst-spread least-used. There is NO codexbar integration (the term appears nowhere). z.ai is the major gap: zai-coding/zai-api are tracked as pool accounts and key-validated, but NO sessionPct/weeklyPct is ever fetched for z.ai (no pollZaiUsage) — its usage probe only stamps {refreshedAt}, and z.ai/Kimi/GLM are deliberately not coding-agent selector candidates (no first-party CLI). Coverage is strong at the unit/offline-e2e level; live switching against ≥2 real accounts exists but is gated/opt-in and produces no captured trajectory/screenshot/video evidence in-repo.

existing.component: AccountPool selection brain (priority/round-robin/least-used/quota-aware, session affinity, burst-spread, health filtering, CRUD, mutations, keep-alive sweep)
existing.whatItDoes: Owns which LinkedAccountConfig serves a request. applyStrategy() implements all 4 strategies (quota-aware skips accounts with sessionPct>=85 via QUOTA_AWARE_SKIP_PCT=85; least-used sorts by usage.sessionPct then recency). filterEligible() excludes disabled/excluded/non-OK accounts and re-admits rate-limited ones past their reset. markRateLimited/markNeedsReauth/markInvalid/markHealthy mutate health. refreshUsage() routes to pollAnthropicUsage/pollCodexUsage; explicitly returns (no probe) for direct-API providers. sweepAccountPoolKeepAlive() refreshes subscription usage every ~5min; startAccountPoolKeepAlive wired in runtime boot.

existing.component: Live usage probes + JSONL day counters
existing.whatItDoes: pollAnthropicUsage() GETs https://api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage and parses five_hour (sessionPct) + seven_day (weeklyPct) utilization (handles flat + nested shapes). pollCodexUsage() GETs https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage parsing rate_limit.primary_window.used_percent (sessionPct only; Codex has no weekly). recordCall() appends per-(provider,account,day) JSONL; readTodayCounters() aggregates calls/tokens/errors. NO z.ai/Kimi probe exists.

Installs globalThis bridge. AGENT_PROVIDER_CANDIDATES maps claude→[anthropic-subscription,anthropic-api], codex→[openai-codex,openai-api], opencode→[cerebras-api]; z.ai/Kimi/GLM intentionally absent. select() picks account, resolves token, materializes CODEX_HOME/auth.json+config.toml (TOML-injection guarded), returns envPatch. isAuthFailure() triages 401/403 vs transient. recordUsage/markRateLimited/markNeedsReauth delegate to pool.

Reads the globalThis bridge without importing app-core. isMultiAccountAgentType (claude/codex/opencode only). selectCodingAccount() never throws, returns selection+non-secret meta. accountMetaFromSessionMetadata() parses stamped account. MULTI_ACCOUNT_AGENT_TYPES excludes zai/glm/kimi.

spawnSession() (around line 566-602) resolves strategy from ELIZA_CODING_ACCOUNT_STRATEGY, calls selectCodingAccount with sessionKey=session id, merges envPatch into customCredentials, stamps account meta, logs the selected account.

existing.component: Usage attribution + health-driven switching + account overview
existing.whatItDoes: recordUsage() (811) sums per-turn tokens into the serving account via bridge.recordUsage. error/login_required handlers (641-690) call markSessionAccountUnhealthy → bridge.markRateLimited/markNeedsReauth (auth on 401/403, rate-limit on 429/quota; 529 deliberately excluded). getAccountOverview()/getRoomRoster() build the dashboard DTOs (strategy + availability + per-session assignments).

existing.component: Accounts REST API incl. z.ai handling + refresh-usage
existing.whatItDoes: /api/accounts CRUD; /refresh-usage drives pool.refreshUsage for OAuth subscription providers (canonical %), falling back to inline 1-token probes. probeCodingPlanKey (zai-coding) and probeDirectApiKey (zai-api) only GET /models to validate the key and set usage:{refreshedAt} — no sessionPct/weeklyPct for z.ai. Keeps zai-coding and zai-api in separate credential pools.

Renders per-account 5h (sessionPct) + 7d (weeklyPct) usage bars (color by 60/85 thresholds), health dot, in-use count, strategy badge, availability chips, and per-room roster. UsageBar returns null when pct undefined — so z.ai accounts (no %) show no usage bar.

tests.covers: Provider-scoped resolution on id collision; scoped health mutations; refreshUsage parsing Codex used_percent→sessionPct; priority/round-robin/session-affinity selection; burst-spread least-used across 3 equal-usage accounts; quota-aware skips sessionPct>=85; least-used picks lowest sessionPct.
tests.gaps: No weeklyPct selection test; no Anthropic usage-parse test (only Codex); no z.ai; no test that filterEligible re-admits a rate-limited account after its reset window; no concurrency/burst beyond 3.

tests.covers: least-used Claude → CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN; per-account CODEX_HOME/auth.json incl id_token; ELIZA_CODEX_MODEL TOML-injection guard; opencode→cerebras least-used→CEREBRAS_API_KEY; per-account usage delta via recordUsage; null when no accounts; rate-limited account skipped; describe() availability; isAuthFailure triage table.

tests.covers: Keystone: bridge consulted on spawn; Claude token injected + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY dropped; Codex per-account CODEX_HOME + OPENAI_API_KEY/OPENAI_MODEL dropped; opencode CEREBRAS_API_KEY; single-account fallback when no bridge; non-multi-account types skip bridge; two consecutive claude spawns land on DISTINCT accounts (rotating mock).

Asserts 10 checks: two claude spawns pick distinct accounts, each child gets distinct CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, parent ANTHROPIC/OPENAI keys dropped, Codex gets per-account CODEX_HOME with right token+account_id.

tests.covers: Imports real ~/.codex login as pooled account, probes REAL session usage (logs sessionPct + resetsAt), bridge selects it + materializes CODEX_HOME, spawns a REAL Codex sub-agent that builds LIVE_PROOF.txt; reports rate-limit honestly. Spends real quota.

gaps.title: z.ai (and Kimi/GLM) usage % is never fetched or tracked — only key validation
gaps.description: For zai-coding (probeCodingPlanKey) and zai-api (probeDirectApiKey) in accounts-routes.ts, the 'refresh usage' path only does GET /models to validate the key and writes usage:{refreshedAt} with NO sessionPct/weeklyPct. AccountPool.refreshUsage explicitly returns without probing for any non-subscription provider, and there is no pollZaiUsage anywhere. So the quota-aware/least-used strategies treat every z.ai account as sessionPct=0 (a.usage?.sessionPct ?? 0), the dashboard shows no usage bars for z.ai, and z.ai cannot be load-balanced by remaining quota. The prompt's question 'is z.ai tracked' answers: accounts are tracked, usage% is NOT.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add pollZaiUsage (and pollMoonshotUsage) to account-usage.ts hitting z.ai's quota/balance endpoint (or parse z.ai rate-limit response headers in probeDirectApiKey/probeCodingPlanKey into sessionPct), wire into AccountPool.refreshUsage for zai-api/zai-coding/moonshot-api, and add a unit test asserting sessionPct is populated. If z.ai exposes no usage endpoint, document that limitation explicitly and have the widget show 'usage unavailable' rather than a silent empty bar.

gaps.title: Mid-task live account SWITCHING (rate-limit → re-select a different account) is not tested live or with captured evidence
gaps.description: The exclude-on-failure design (P0 hitlist 'mark account + re-select excluding it') is realized only as health flagging: on 429/quota orchestrator-task-service.markSessionAccountUnhealthy marks the account rate-limited so the NEXT select() skips it (filterEligible). There is no test that an in-flight sub-agent whose account hits 429 actually re-spawns/continues on a different account, and no live trajectory/timeline proving a real switch under real quota exhaustion. The live tests only prove distinct accounts are handed out across fresh spawns via the exclude set.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add an integration test that injects a 429 'error' event for a running session and asserts (a) markRateLimited is called for that account, (b) a subsequent spawn for the same agentType selects a different account, and (c) the original is re-admitted after its reset. domain: Orchestrator/task UI surfaces (plugins/plugin-task-coordinator) — OrchestratorWorkbench, CodingAgentTasksPanel, PTY consoles, orchestrator-stream/diff/plan/reasoning/markdown chat rendering, TaskCardList, CodingAgentSettingsSection
summary: plugin-task-coordinator is the display/control layer for coding-agent task threads and PTY sessions; it owns zero server runtime (all state lives in @elizaos/plugin-agent-orchestrator) and renders entirely as views + slot fills. The chat/coding UX is mature and well-built: OrchestratorWorkbench.tsx (3925 lines) renders a Claude-Code/Codex-style flowing conversation (user bubbles, flat agent markdown, structured tool-call cards with diffs/output, collapsible reasoning, plan/acceptance/usage inspector, recovery actions like retry/rerun/restart-with-edited-plan), driven by SSE streaming + debounced tail-refetch + active-poll. CodingAgentTasksPanel is the simpler task-coordinator landing; PtyConsoleBase/Drawer/SidePanel/TerminalPane render live PTY output (raw <pre> buffer cap 200k, plus an xterm.js pane). The conversation-builder transform (orchestrator-stream.helpers) is heavily unit-tested (118 unit tests pass overall). HOWEVER: the live e2e (test/orchestrator-workbench.live.e2e.test.ts) is STALE — it asserts test IDs that no longer exist in source (orchestrator-task-item, orchestrator-composer, orchestrator-new-task, orchestrator-create-dialog), so it is a no-op even when a stack is up. The screenshot harness (run-dashboard-shot.mjs) only shoots static TaskCard fixtures (not the real workbench/PTY/settings), its output is gitignored, and it is wired into no package.json script. The largest behavioral DOM test (TaskInspector terminal-task guards) currently FAILS TO LOAD (broken @elizaos/shared/voice-eot resolution), so `bun run test` exits non-zero and that suite does not run. No committed screenshot/video/trajectory evidence exists for this domain.

domain: Scenario-runner test harness (packages/scenario-runner) — executor, interceptor, judge, reporter/run-viewer, native-export, final-checks, schema; coding-task/sub-agent capability testing
summary: packages/scenario-runner is a mature, well-structured integration harness: it boots a real PGLite-backed AgentRuntime (live or deterministic-LLM-proxy), drives four turn kinds (message/action/api/tick, plus voice), intercepts every action handler into CapturedAction[] (params/result/error/artifacts/state-transitions/connector-dispatches/memory-writes/approvals), runs ~30 typed finalCheck handlers plus an LLM-as-judge (Cerebras gpt-oss-120b or runtime TEXT_LARGE), and emits a JSON aggregate + per-scenario bundle + a self-contained HTML run-viewer + eliza_native_v1 training-corpus JSONL derived from recorded trajectory files. Trajectory capture works (runtime auto-writes per-stage JSON when ELIZA_TRAJECTORY_DIR is set; reporter summarizes stages/tokens/cache/tool calls into the viewer). For CODING capability it is shallow: exactly ONE coding scenario exists (deterministic-coding-tools-actions: FILE/SHELL/WORKTREE direct action handlers in a seeded git repo), and it explicitly does NOT spawn sub-agents. The whole sub-agent/orchestration surface (plugin-agent-orchestrator TASKS spawning claude-code/codex/opencode over PTY, smithers workflows, sweagent) is excluded from the harness by design (LIVE_ONLY_REMAINDER) and only unit-tested. The harness captures NO real screenshots/video/timeline — the "screenshot"/"frontendScreenshot" artifact fields are just string lengths from mocked computer-use, browser-screenshot scenarios assert a canned string, and real visual capture lives in a separate Playwright lane (packages/app/test/ui-smoke, cloud-frontend e2e) that scenario-pr-workflow.test.ts only checks is wired. No build-validation, no multi-project concurrency, no app-create-and-reload, no rollback, and no multi-model/account-switching scenarios exist.

existing.component: runScenario executor (turn loop, seeds, requires-gate, finalChecks, judge)
existing.whatItDoes: Core execution: resolves rooms, runs seed steps (custom/advanceClock/seed-dispatch), gates on requires.plugins, runs message/action/api/tick/voice turns with per-turn timeout, runs per-turn assertions (assertResponse/assertTurn/responseIncludesAny/forbiddenActions/responseJudge) and finalChecks, synthesizes an implicit non-success REPLY when a message turn emits text but the LLM selected no action, mounts a fake computeruse service, starts an in-process loopback HTTP server for api turns, resolves {{now}}/{{definitionId}}/{{occurrenceId}} template tokens.

Idempotently wraps every runtime action handler to capture actionName/parameters/result/error; wraps createMemory (memoryWrites) and createTask (approval-request inference); derives connector dispatches (MESSAGE/VOICE_CALL), state transitions (browser_task completed/needs_human, intervention), and artifacts (screenshot/frontendScreenshot/path/attachments) — but only as strings/lengths, never real binaries.

~30 typed handlers: actionCalled, selectedAction, selectedActionArguments, memoryWriteOccurred, approvalRequestExists, approvalStateTransition, connectorDispatchOccurred, messageDelivered, draftExists, uploadedAssetExists, browserTaskCompleted/NeedsHuman, noSideEffectOnReject, push*, clarificationRequested, interventionRequestExists, workflowDispatchOccurred, gmail* (mock-ledger backed), custom (predicate), judgeRubric (deferred to executor). Unknown type or unknown field fails loudly via FINAL_CHECK_KEYS strict-key validation.

judgeTextWithLlm scores a candidate (response text + structured action trace) against a rubric 0..1 using Cerebras gpt-oss-120b when enabled else runtime TEXT_LARGE; retry-on-unparseable (2 retries), no heuristic fallback — throws if neither model works. Transport in cerebras-judge.ts.

Builds the aggregate report, writes matrix.json + per-scenario JSON + a self-contained HTML run-viewer (index.html + data.js) that renders scenarios, turn tables, call-by-call trajectory stage summaries (latency/tokens/cache%/tool name+success/preview), and eliza_native_v1 rows. Pure text/JSON; embeds no images or video.

exportScenarioNativeJsonl reads recorded per-stage trajectory JSON under <runDir>/trajectories/, converts each model-boundary stage to an eliza_native_v1 row, stamps each row with the scenario's pass/fail/skip outcome (so failed runs aren't exported as gold), and writes a manifest with counts.

existing.component: schema (turn kinds, finalCheck types, lanes, scenario())
existing.whatItDoes: ScenarioDefinition/ScenarioTurn/CapturedAction types; FINAL_CHECK_KEYS strict-key map; scenario() validator enforcing finalCheck field whitelist + lane validity. ScenarioTurn.kind is an open string but executor only supports message|action|api|tick|voice.

Discovers/loads/filters by lane, single shared runtime per process (PGLite cannot be recreated), sets ELIZA_TRAJECTORY_DIR/ELIZA_LIFEOPS_RUN_ID/ELIZA_LIFEOPS_SCENARIO_ID so the recorder tags trajectories, writes report + run-viewer + native jsonl, enforces SKIP_REASON loud-skip policy and exit codes.

Does NOT itself instantiate a trajectory recorder — that happens in packages/core/src/services/message.ts via ELIZA_TRAJECTORY_DIR.

Tests built-in coding TOOLS, not sub-agent orchestration or build validation.

domain: E2E recording: screenshots, video, timelines (across cloud/desktop/AOSP/Debian/Play/iOS), with focus on recording coding/agent-orchestration flows end-to-end
summary: The repo has a mature, Playwright-based screenshot+video+trace recording stack and a custom contact-sheet/viewer pipeline, but it is built for UI visual auditing, not for recording a real coding/agent-orchestration flow end-to-end. The root `test:e2e:record` (scripts/e2e-recordings/run-all.mjs) runs 9 Playwright suites with E2E_RECORD=1 (video:on, trace:on, screenshot:on), extracts trace frames into per-test contact sheets, and builds an index.html viewer with per-test video links — this is solid for web/cloud/app UI. cloud-frontend's `audit:cloud` (aesthetic-audit.spec.ts) is the most polished surface: full-page desktop+mobile screenshots, hover screenshots, console/network-error capture, palette/radius linting, screenshot-quality gates, and auto-stubbed manual-review markdown per route. The app package has the broadest Playwright matrix (web, mobile-Pixel7, voice-mic, audit-app, electrobun-packaged desktop, android-cdp). The critical gap for THIS audit: there is NO harness that records a real coding session (claude-code/codex/opencode/smithers spawning, file edits, plugin reload, errors, rollback) as video+timeline. The closest spec, orchestrator-gui-workbench.spec.ts, is 100% mocked API; scenario-runner emits JSON reports + native JSONL trajectories but produces zero screenshots/video/timeline. There is also no recording for AOSP/Debian/Play/iOS remote-container flows (only a USB-installer wizard Playwright suite and an electrobun desktop screenshot assertion). All recorded output is gitignored (e2e-recordings/, test-results/, aesthetic-audit-output/), so no committed evidence exists for coding flows; .github/issue-evidence holds only two ad-hoc PNGs.

existing.whatItDoes: Runs the 9 UI_E2E_SUITES (suites.mjs) with E2E_RECORD=1, writing Playwright output into e2e-recordings/<pkg>/test-results, then invokes contact-sheet + viewer generators. Supports --packages, --skip-tests/sheets/viewer. This is the canonical full screenshot+video+trace capture entry point.

Declares the recordable suites (app, cloud-frontend, cloud-e2e, homepage, os-homepage, os-usb-installer, ui-agent-surface, feed-dag-visualizer) plus covered-by/skipped lists. Each entry has configDir/script/recordEnv. Notably the app suite is forced into stub mode (ELIZA_UI_SMOKE_FORCE_STUB=1) so recordings are deterministic, not real.

Scans test-results for Playwright trace.zip files, extracts *-trace.trace, pulls non-blank screenshot frames into contact-sheets/<pkg>/<test>/frames/, discovers .webm video files, and writes e2e-recordings/manifest.json with {video, frames} per test. Skips tests with no trace or video.

Reads manifest.json and builds e2e-recordings/index.html — a per-test card grid with a Video link (or 'no video') and frame thumbnails. This is the closest thing to a 'run viewer' for UI recordings; it has no notion of an agent/coding timeline.

Walks ~60 cloud routes at desktop(1440x900)+mobile(390x844): full-page screenshot, --hover.png after hovering primary button, live :hover color sampling, palette(blue-ban, orange<->black)+border-radius linting, console-error + failed-request capture, sharp-based screenshot-quality gate, and auto-stubs manual-review/<slug>.md. Outputs report.json + contact-sheet.html. Auth via synthetic injected JWT + full /api/* route mocking.

Defines projects: chromium, chromium-voice-mic (real WAV fed as fake mic), mobile-chromium (Pixel 7), audit-app (all-views aesthetic audit). Honors E2E_RECORD to flip trace/video/screenshot to 'on' and redirect outputDir into e2e-recordings/app. Boots the live app stack via app-core/scripts/playwright-ui-live-stack.ts.

Drives the coding-task orchestrator surface: renders build-room with codex/cerebras sessions, inspector controls, retry/stop session, orchestrator-timeline assertions. Records video/trace via the ui-smoke config. BUT every /api/orchestrator/* call is mocked (taskSummary/taskDetail/timelinePage fixtures) — it captures the UI chrome of a coding flow, never a real sub-agent run.

App-side analogue of the cloud aesthetic audit: walks ~50 app views x 2 viewports capturing screenshots; run via the dedicated audit-app project. Covers the app shell visually but not coding-flow dynamics.

Captures per-route screenshots (desktop/mobile, light/dark) with a quality-retry helper and a button inventory, writing captures/<route>/ + a report under reports/ai-qa. trace/video are explicitly OFF here (screenshot-only inventory).

Drives the built desktop app binary (no webServer). trace/video/screenshot are retain-on-failure only. desktop-launch-render.e2e.spec.ts takes a real window screenshot via the harness bridge (/main-window/screenshot) and asserts non-blank. This is the only desktop-shell recording, and it is a single render assertion, not a flow video.

Runs Playwright over the Android WebView CDP socket (screenshot/trace retain-on-failure; notes they are slow over CDP). android-e2e.mjs handles emulator boot, APK install, voice-model staging via adb. No screenrecord/scrcpy video capture; recording on Android is effectively absent.
Boots local cloud API + frontend with auth-cookie login and provisioning flows; honors recording flag to set trace/screenshot/video to 'on'. Feeds the cloud-e2e suite in the recording orchestrator.

Enumerates every live/real/e2e package script and flags LLM-shaped ones lacking artifact evidence (recognizing scenario/e2e:record/remote-capabilities artifact patterns). Encodes the policy that UI suites yield screenshots/traces while model calls yield trajectory artifacts — but does not itself require coding-flow video/timeline.

This is the mandated real-LLM trajectory evidence per CLAUDE.md, but it produces NO screenshots, video, or visual timeline — purely textual turn/assertion data.

domain: Dynamic plugin/view reload, build validation, undo/rollback, broken-reload recovery
summary: elizaOS has a solid build-validation pipeline (AppVerificationService: real typecheck/lint/test/build/launch/browser, structured-proof gating, per-check logs + screenshot) and a partial in-process runtime reload path. Reload is two-layered: (1) dev-only process bounce in packages/app-core/scripts/dev-ui.mjs (a source watcher that restarts the API child, not in-process HMR), and (2) a genuine in-process lifecycle: loadPluginFromDirectory cache-busts the ESM import and calls runtime.registerPlugin (which the plugin-lifecycle wrapper extends to register/unregister Plugin.views), exposed at POST /api/plugins/load-from-directory and invoked by VerificationRoomBridgeService after a passing verdict; plus applyPluginRuntimeMutation (config_apply -> plugin_reload -> runtime_reload -> restart_required tiers) and a runtime-ops manager with hot/warm/cold strategies. View bundles are served from disk (GET /api/views/:id/bundle.js) with mtime+size ETag and content-hash cache-busting, so a rebuilt bundle is picked up on next browser fetch. The big gaps: there is NO rollback/undo for code or runtime state — a broken live-load is surfaced honestly ('built and verified but live-load failed; reload the agent') but never reverted; applyPluginRuntimeMutation can unload plugins then fail mid-reload and fall back to a full restart without restoring the prior in-memory graph; and the cold strategy swaps the runtime before the health check with the manager explicitly noting that restoring the previous runtime would require a 'two-phase restart contract' that does not exist. There is no git-revert/snapshot of edited source — the only 'undo' in the codebase is the homescreen canvas history (a client-side scene-document op stack), which is unrelated to plugin/view code rollback. Evidence capture (trajectory/screenshot/video/timeline) specific to reload/rollback is essentially absent.

existing.whatItDoes: Runs a structured verification pipeline (typecheck/lint/test/build/launch/browser/structured-proof) against an app/plugin workdir via execFile, persists each check's full log + a Puppeteer screenshot to a verification dir, parses tsc/eslint/vitest output into diagnostics, validates a structured completion proof against actual check results, and returns a pass/fail verdict + retryablePromptForChild for the orchestrator to retry the coding agent.

Resolves a plugin's built JS entry (package.json module/main/exports -> dist/index.js), dynamically imports it with a ?t=Date.now() cache-buster so a rebuilt module re-evaluates, and calls runtime.registerPlugin (lifecycle wrapper also registers Plugin.views) so an edited+rebuilt plugin's actions/providers/views appear without an agent restart. unload delegates to runtime.unloadPlugin. Path-traversal/symlink-escape guarded. NOTE: it is explicitly NOT a build step — caller must have compiled first.

HTTP entry (lines ~2496-2580) that gates on isLocalCodeExecutionAllowed (store-variant block), validates an absolute directory, and delegates to loadPluginFromDirectory/unloadPluginFromDirectory. Returns 422 with the import error on failure. This is the wire the VerificationRoomBridge calls over loopback.

Subscribes to the SwarmCoordinator broadcast bus; on a passing PLUGIN_CREATE verdict it POSTs the workdir to /api/plugins/load-from-directory (loadPluginFromWorkdir) and posts a chat message: 'built, verified, and loaded live' on success, or 'built and verified ... but live-load failed: <err>. Reload the agent to pick it up.' on failure. Honest failure surfacing but no rollback.

Diffs previous vs next resolved plugin graphs and applies the lightest change: in-place applyPluginConfig (config_apply), else unload+registerPlugin (plugin_reload), else full runtime restart (runtime_reload/restart_required) when a plugin has a DB adapter or lifecycle is unsupported. On a mid-reload throw it logs and falls back to restart — but does NOT restore plugins it already unloaded (no rollback of the in-memory graph).

DefaultRuntimeOperationManager: single-flight, idempotency-keyed, phase-logged operation runner. Classifies intents into hot/warm/cold tiers, runs the strategy, then a health-check gate. createHotStrategy (reload-hot.ts) mutates env + best-effort plugin.applyConfig (~100ms, no reload). createColdStrategy delegates to an injected restartRuntime closure. CRITICAL: on a failed post-swap health check the manager comments it cannot restore the previous runtime ('requires a two-phase restart contract') — so cold reload has no rollback.

existing.component: View bundle build + disk serving with cache-busting
existing.whatItDoes: GET /api/views/:id/bundle.js serves the compiled view bundle from disk (getBundleDiskPath) with an mtime+size-derived ETag, 304 support, content-hash (?v=bundleHash) immutable caching when versioned else no-cache so a rebuilt bundle is revalidated/picked up. View bundles themselves are built by per-plugin Vite configs (vite.config.views.ts) producing dist/views/bundle.js; the runtime does not rebuild them — the coding agent's build step does.

startAgentSourceWatcher watches */src dirs and bounces the API child via the supervisor when source changes, only when the agent is healthy, skipping bulk changes (>HOT_RELOAD_BULK_CHANGE_LIMIT, default 4) that look like a git reset/build. This is a full process restart (dev convenience), NOT in-process HMR, and is not the runtime reload path used in production.

existing.component: Plugin import staging (ESM module-graph cache busting on re-import)
existing.whatItDoes: importPluginModuleFromPath stages a plugin into <stateDir>/plugins/.runtime-imports/<pkg>/<mkdtemp> on any re-import of an already-imported package name, so a mutated plugin gets a fresh staged URL that busts ESM's un-evictable module graph. Prunes stale staging dirs (keep newest N). Cold fast-path imports in place on first import. Underpins hot-reload correctness.

existing.component: VIEWS/APP create+edit dispatch -> coding agent + verification validator
existing.whatItDoes: Multi-turn flow: scaffolds from min-plugin template (or locates existing source for edit), dispatches START_CODING_TASK with validator {service:'app-verification',method:'verifyPlugin',profile:'full'} and onVerificationFail:'retry', links a trajectory, and stamps originRoomId so the bridge can post the verdict. Ties together build-validate-reload but does not itself trigger the live reload (that is the bridge).

existing.component: Plugin lifecycle teardown (closest thing to per-plugin rollback)
existing.whatItDoes: installRuntimePluginLifecycle wraps registerPlugin/unloadPlugin to track owned actions/providers/evaluators/services/routes/events/models/views and tear them down cleanly. restoreAdapterIfNeeded restores a prior DB adapter on unload. This is component-level cleanup, NOT a snapshot/rollback of a failed reload — there is no save-before / restore-after of the whole plugin graph.

tests.covers: load+register+unload of a built dir plugin; entry resolution (package.json main -> dist/index.js fallback); views-bearing plugin registers without throwing; path-traversal and symlink-escape rejection; clear error when no built entry; error when export is not a plugin.
tests.gaps: Does NOT exercise the actual hot-reload semantic (load same dir twice after editing the file and assert the NEW code is live via ?t= cache-bust). No broken-reload recovery (load a plugin whose import throws mid-evaluation and assert the runtime is left in a consistent prior state). No concurrency (two reloads racing). No rollback.

tests.covers: verify->live-load->chat-verdict path: posts 'loaded live' on pass (asserts POST to /api/plugins/load-from-directory, never 'reinject'); 'build-passed-but-load-failed' honest verdict when load route returns 422 'import threw: bad export'; fail verdict with summary; boot-order attach retries; dedupe; stop() teardown.
tests.gaps: Mocks fetch — never drives a REAL broken plugin through the real load route. Asserts only the chat TEXT on broken load, not that the runtime graph is unchanged/recovered. No assertion that a failed load did not partially register actions/views. No rollback verification.

tests.covers: AppVerificationService.verifyApp against a real temp project using real tsc for typecheck; pass and fail TS fixtures; lint/test wired to local shims; fast profile verdict. Gated on bun/npm availability.
tests.gaps: No real launch/browser/screenshot step (those are unit-mocked elsewhere). No build-then-reload end-to-end. No structured-proof-against-checks integration. Does not feed a verified plugin into loadPluginFromDirectory.

tests.covers: Cold fast-path vs staging: flag on + same package name imported twice -> 2nd import goes through staging (the ESM module-graph cache-bust that makes hot-reload pick up mutated code).
tests.gaps: Tests the staging decision, not an actual mutate-then-reimport-then-assert-new-behavior reload. No failure/rollback path.

tests.covers: Manager classifies provider-switch from the pre-prepare provider snapshot (ensures an unloaded provider plugin gets a cold restart rather than a hot no-op).

gaps.title: No rollback/recovery on a broken plugin reload (in-memory graph can be left half-torn-down)
gaps.description: applyPluginRuntimeMutation (plugin-runtime-apply.ts:286-334) unloads removed/reload packages, then registers new ones; if registerPlugin throws mid-loop it logs and falls back to a full restart but never restores the plugins it already unloaded. loadPluginFromDirectory has no snapshot/restore either. The runtime-ops cold strategy (manager.ts:269-278) swaps the runtime BEFORE the health check and the code explicitly states it cannot restore the previous runtime ('requires a two-phase restart contract'). A broken reload of an edited plugin/view can therefore leave the live agent with missing actions/providers/views until a manual full restart.
gaps.suggestedWork: Implement a two-phase reload: snapshot the plugin ownership graph (and adapter) before unload/register, and on any failure (register throw OR post-reload health-check failure) restore the snapshot (re-register the previous module, restore adapter, re-register prior views). Add the two-phase restart contract the manager comment references so cold reload can revert to the prior runtime. Add unit tests in packages/agent/src/api/plugin-runtime-apply.test.ts and packages/agent/src/runtime/operations covering mid-reload throw and failed-health-check rollback.

gaps.title: plugin-runtime-apply.ts (the core tiered reload pipeline) has zero tests
gaps.description: The central function deciding config_apply vs plugin_reload vs runtime_reload vs restart_required — including the adapter-requires-restart escalation and the mid-reload failure fallback — is completely untested. There is no plugin-runtime-apply.test.ts anywhere.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a unit suite that: applies a config-only change (config_apply), adds/removes a plugin (plugin_reload via unload+register), forces an adapter plugin (runtime_reload escalation), and injects a registerPlugin throw to assert the restart fallback and (once implemented) rollback. Use a fake AgentRuntime with the lifecycle wrapper installed.

gaps.title: No end-to-end edit -> rebuild -> live-reload -> assert-new-behavior test
gaps.description: load-plugin-from-directory.test.ts proves load/unload and security, and plugin-resolver-cold-import proves staging decisions, but nothing edits a built plugin on disk, re-imports it, and asserts the NEW code path is live (the whole point of the ?t= cache-bust + staging). The hot-reload semantic is asserted only by comments.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a test that scaffolds a built plugin returning v1, loads it, rewrites dist/index.js to return v2, calls loadPluginFromDirectory again, and asserts the runtime now serves v2 (action handler / views metadata changed). Add a sibling for a re-import whose module evaluation throws and assert the prior v1 plugin is still registered and functional.

gaps.title: Broken-reload recovery is only asserted via mocked chat text, never a real broken plugin
gaps.description: verification-room-bridge.test.ts mocks fetch to return a 422 and asserts the chat message says 'live-load failed'. No test drives a genuinely broken built plugin (e.g. import that throws, missing export, bad view bundle) through the real /api/plugins/load-from-directory route and asserts (a) the runtime graph is unchanged and (b) the view registry didn't half-register the broken view.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add an integration test that POSTs a real broken plugin dir to the load route and asserts HTTP 422 + no partial registration (actions/providers/views unchanged). Pair with the rollback work above.

gaps.title: View-bundle rebuild-and-reserve is untested at the route level
gaps.description: A modified view's bundle is served from disk with an mtime+size ETag and content-hash cache-bust, but no test verifies that rebuilding dist/views/bundle.js changes the ETag/bundleHash and that a client revalidation returns the new bytes (vs a stale 304). For dynamic view editing this is the browser-side reload contract.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a views-routes bundle test: serve a bundle, capture ETag, rewrite the bundle file (new mtime/size), and assert a fresh request returns 200 with new bytes and a new ETag, and that ?v=<oldHash> vs ?v=<newHash> cache headers differ (immutable vs no-cache).

gaps.title: No real-LLM trajectory / video / before-after screenshot evidence for the reload+rollback flow
gaps.description: Per the repo Definition of Done, agent/action changes need real-LLM trajectories and UI changes need before/after screenshots + video. The create->verify->reload->broken-reload domain has only per-verification logs/screenshots; no captured scenario trajectory, run viewer report, video walkthrough, or before/after screenshot pair exists for it.
gaps.suggestedWork: Author a scenario-runner scenario (packages/scenario-runner) that drives VIEWS create -> coding agent -> verification -> live-load (pass) and a second variant that injects a build/import failure -> honest verdict, capturing JSON report + jsonl + run viewer. Add a test:e2e:record walkthrough showing the view appearing live after edit and the failure message on a broken reload.

gaps.title: No git-revert / source snapshot / undo for code changes in the coding-orchestration context
gaps.description: There is no rollback of edited SOURCE when a verification or reload fails — the coding agent edits in place and a failed verify just prompts a retry; a failed live-load tells the user to 'reload the agent'. The only 'undo' in the searched code is the homescreen canvas history (client-side scene-document op stack in homescreen.ts), which is unrelated to plugin/view code. There is no git stash/branch/checkpoint per coding task to revert a bad edit set.
gaps.suggestedWork: Decide whether per-task source rollback is in scope; if so, add a git checkpoint (branch/stash or commit) before the coding agent edits and a revert path on a final verification/reload failure, surfaced as an orchestrator action. Add tests for checkpoint-create and revert-on-failure.

gaps.title: No concurrency test for parallel plugin reloads (relevant to the 10-project concurrency goal)
gaps.description: The plugin staging pruner and importedPluginPackageNames set guard concurrent imports, and the runtime-ops manager single-flights operations, but loadPluginFromDirectory (the live-load route) has no single-flight gate and no test for two reloads of the same/different plugins racing. The 10-concurrent-projects scenario could hit register/unregister interleaving.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a concurrency test that fires N simultaneous loadPluginFromDirectory calls (same and distinct plugins) and asserts the final registry is consistent and no staging dir is corrupted; add a per-plugin single-flight lock to the live-load route if interleaving is observed.

domain: Platform coverage for coding agents (cloud / desktop / AOSP / Debian / Google Play / iOS, plus remote-container delegation)
summary: elizaOS has two real, distinct coding-execution substrates and a clean per-platform gate between them. (1) Local subprocess/PTY execution via @elizaos/plugin-agent-orchestrator, which spawns ACP coding agents (claude/codex/opencode/elizaos/pi-agent) and is gated at plugin-construction time by isLocalCodeExecutionAllowed() (direct build only) AND detectOrchestratorTerminalSupport() (shell + runtime-mode checks). (2) Remote-container execution via E2BRemoteCapabilityRouterService (providers: e2b, eliza-cloud, home) which routes fs/pty/git capabilities over HTTP to a hosted runner (packages/cloud/services/coding-remote-runner), with the cloud control plane provisioning coding containers (packages/cloud/api/v1/coding-containers + cloud-shared coding-containers service + agent-sandboxes repo). Platform reality: desktop (Electrobun, direct build) and cloud both run local execution with fs access; STORE builds (Mac App Store/MS Store/Flathub) are hard-blocked to a TASKS stub; iOS is blocked entirely (no shell) and is expected to delegate to remote containers via the same agent runtime; Android/AOSP direct builds can run local execution only with local-yolo mode + a staged /system/bin/sh, otherwise stub. IMPORTANT framing correction: plugin-remote-manifest host-shim subpaths, plugin-worker-runtime, and plugin-remote-manifest are NOT coding-agent infrastructure — they are the wire bridge for remote-mode plugin VIEW bundles / remote plugin execution; they live under packages/ (not plugins/) and are unrelated to running coding agents. Maturity is partial-to-solid in source and unit/integration tests, but there is essentially NO captured cross-platform evidence (no trajectory/screenshot/video/timeline of a coding agent actually creating/modifying code on AOSP/Debian/iOS/Play, nor of the iOS->remote-container delegation end-to-end).

domain: Domain J — Model backends for coding/orchestration (model routing for built-in vs sub-agent coding paths; gpt-oss-120b/Cerebras, claude, codex, z.ai wiring across cloud-routing, plugin-elizacloud, plugin-anthropic(+proxy), plugin-codex-cli, plugin-cli-inference, plugin-openai/openrouter, plugin-agent-orchestrator)
summary: The model-backend layer for coding/orchestration is mature and well-architected, with a clear separation between two paths. (1) The BUILT-IN path (agent answers/plans itself) routes through `runtime.useModel(ModelType.*)`; the eliza-cloud built-in coding model is gpt-oss-120b, wired as the default TEXT_SMALL via `DEFAULT_ELIZA_CLOUD_TEXT_MODEL = "gpt-oss-120b"` in packages/core/src/contracts/service-routing.ts and plugin-elizacloud's `getSmallModel`; the cloud gateway transparently routes gpt-oss to Cerebras (detected by `isCerebrasServedModel`). Large/planner tier defaults to `zai-glm-4.7` (z.ai). plugin-openai independently supports a direct Cerebras mode (CEREBRAS_API_KEY/base-url). (2) The SUB-AGENT path spawns external coding CLIs (elizaos, pi-agent, opencode, codex, claude) as ACP subprocesses; model selection is per-agent-type in AcpService.buildEnv (sets OPENAI_MODEL/ANTHROPIC_MODEL/OPENCODE_MODEL), opencode resolves provider+model via buildOpencodeSpawnConfig (Cerebras gpt-oss-120b default), and a multi-account credential bridge (coding-account-selection.ts) rotates pooled accounts for claude/codex/opencode. cloud/routing/resolve.ts is the local-key-vs-cloud-proxy-vs-disabled decider but is generic and NOT itself coding-aware. Unit coverage is strong (multi-account spawn rotation, opencode auto-detect, codex auth/backend, cerebras response_format gate, cloud-routing resolve). LIVE-model coverage exists but is narrow and entirely opt-in (gated by CEREBRAS_API_KEY+ELIZA_RUN_LIVE_TESTS, RUN_LIVE_NATIVE_ACP, RUN_LIVE_ACPX) — no committed trajectory/screenshot/video/timeline evidence ties a real coding task end-to-end to a chosen model, and there is no live test that asserts an actual sub-agent COMPLETES a coding task on gpt-oss-120b vs claude vs codex with captured artifacts.

existing.component: gpt-oss-120b as eliza-cloud built-in coding model (default TEXT_SMALL)
existing.whatItDoes: Defines DEFAULT_ELIZA_CLOUD_TEXT_MODEL = "gpt-oss-120b" (line 46), re-exported from core index.node.ts. This is the canonical default the elizacloud plugin's getSmallModel falls back to, making gpt-oss-120b the built-in small/nano/medium/response-handler model for cloud-connected agents.

existing.component: elizacloud text model handlers + Cerebras/gpt-oss detection
existing.whatItDoes: Implements TEXT_NANO/SMALL/MEDIUM/LARGE/MEGA/RESPONSE_HANDLER/ACTION_PLANNER handlers. isCerebrasServedModel() (line 359) detects gpt-oss by name and downgrades response_format json_schema->json_object since Cerebras 400s on json_schema. extractCostUsd reads gateway USD. Priority-50 plugin wins the default text slot.

getSmallModel falls back to gpt-oss-120b (via DEFAULT_ELIZA_CLOUD_TEXT_MODEL); getLargeModel/MegaModel/ActionPlanner fall back to zai-glm-4.7 (DEFAULT_ELIZA_CLOUD_LARGE_MODEL, line 5). nano/medium fall back to small. Reads ELIZAOS_CLOUD_*_MODEL then bare *_MODEL env aliases.

existing.component: cloud-routing resolver (local-key vs cloud-proxy vs disabled)
existing.whatItDoes: resolveCloudRoute/resolveFeatureCloudRoute pick a route source per RouteSpec; isCloudConnected gates on ELIZAOS_CLOUD_API_KEY + ELIZAOS_CLOUD_ENABLED. Generic per-feature policy (local/cloud/auto). NOTE: not coding-specific — it is the transport router, not the model chooser for sub-agents.

domain: Domain K — Surfacing coding tasks to users + connectors (home-screen widget + notifications, shortcuts, slash commands, actions surfaced, rollout to Telegram/Discord/social connectors; update cadence / progress modes)
summary: Coding-task surfacing is implemented almost entirely inside plugin-agent-orchestrator. registerProgressHook (src/index.ts) is the single hot path: it subscribes to AcpService session events and posts narration/heartbeat to the sub-agent's origin room (Discord channel, Telegram chat/thread, etc.). The "don't be annoying" cadence is real and centralized — resolveSubAgentProgressPolicy parses ACPX_PROGRESS_MODE (compact|threaded|silent, plus an undocumented "ack" mode) and ACPX_PROGRESS_DELAY_MS (default 15s, capped 120s) with ACPX_PROGRESS_REACTIONS, falling back to process.env when getSetting misses; a capability-aware routing ladder (thread → edit-in-place → fresh send) plus an LLM heartbeat (10s fast / 30s slow) with dedupe keeps the channel clean. On task_complete, sub-agent-router.ts also fans the result out to the cross-platform AgentNotification surface (category "agent", deepLink "/orchestrator"), which the UI notification-store delivers to in-app toast, desktop OS, and mobile local notifications. Connectors expose the needed primitives via MessageConnector capabilities: Discord has all five (send/react/edit/create_thread/post_to_thread), Telegram has send/create_thread/post_to_thread but not edit/react. Slash commands/shortcuts (plugin-commands) surface /subagents but no coding-task-specific shortcut, and client commands are filtered off chat connectors by design. Maturity is partial: the policy logic is well unit-tested and the production code is thorough, but the actual posting/heartbeat/notification surfacing is largely untested and has zero trajectory/screenshot/video/timeline evidence. The native plugins (mobile-signals, eliza-tasks, reminders) are generic device bridges, not coding-task surfaces.

existing.component: Sub-agent progress hook (cadence engine + connector posting)
existing.whatItDoes: registerProgressHook (L729) subscribes to AcpService events and posts progress to the origin room. emitProgress (L1131) is the routing ladder: per-session thread → edit-in-place main message → fresh send, all capability-gated via hasCap/getMessageConnectors. Adds 🚀/✅/❌ reactions when supported, caches one main message + one thread per (source,roomId,label), dedupes spawn ACKs per session and per room (ACK_ROOM_DEDUP_MS=60s), and wraps runtime.sendMessageToTarget to redirect planner chatter into the per-task thread.

existing.component: Progress-mode policy resolution (the 'don't be annoying' control)
existing.whatItDoes: resolveSubAgentProgressPolicy (L532) + parseProgressMode/parseProgressDelayMs/parseProgressReactions (L499-529) read ACPX_PROGRESS_MODE / ELIZA_SUB_AGENT_PROGRESS_MODE (compact|threaded|silent|ack), ACPX_PROGRESS_DELAY_MS (default 15000, clamped 0..120000), ACPX_PROGRESS_REACTIONS. 'silent' suppresses all posts; 'ack' posts one spawn ACK and forces delayMs=0; default 'compact' debounces 15s and strips emoji/labels. Falls back to process.env when runtime.getSetting misses (env-only config bug fix).

startHeartbeat (L972) runs every 10s (edit-capable surfaces) or 30s (post-only) while a session is non-terminal, summarizing recent narration+tool-calls via ModelType.TEXT_SMALL (HEARTBEAT_SUMMARY_PROMPT, L649), skipping when there is nothing concrete and deduping identical consecutive lines — explicitly preferring silence over a repeated 'still working' line.

On task_complete (L831-854) calls getNotifier(runtime).notify({title:'<label> finished', body:<preview>, category:'agent', priority:'normal', source:'orchestrator', deepLink:'/orchestrator', groupKey:'orchestrator:<sessionId>'}). getNotifier (L1375) resolves ServiceType.NOTIFICATION. This is the push-to-user path for notifications/home surfaces.

Defines AgentNotification with categories including 'task' (task-coordinator/scheduled completion) and 'agent' (background/coding agent finished), priority, deepLink, groupKey. notification-store.ts (packages/ui) ingests and fans out to in-app toast, desktop OS (Electrobun), and mobile local notifications; native-notifications.ts bridges to @capacitor/local-notifications, ElizaIntent, or web Notification.
TASKS:create mints a durable task thread and emits [TASK:<threadId>]<title>[/TASK] in the callback text (L724-727) so the chat surface renders a TaskWidget linking back to the workbench.

Orchestrator declares three chat-sidebar widgets (agent-orchestrator.apps/accounts/activity, src/index.ts L183-211). plugin-task-coordinator renders OrchestratorWorkbench, CodingAgentTasksPanel, and a view 'interact' capability handler (CodingAgentTasksPanel.interact.ts) that calls client.getCodingAgentStatus / listCodingAgentTaskThreads.

Registers MessageConnector with capabilities send_message/resolve_targets/list_rooms/chat_context/user_context/create_thread/post_to_thread (L54-62). Notably lacks edit_message and react_message, so compact-mode progress falls to fresh-send and reactions are unavailable on Telegram.
Declares send_message/react_message/edit_message/create_thread/post_to_thread (L318-335), so the orchestrator's full routing ladder (edit-in-place, reactions, threaded narration) works on Discord.

Builds explicit slash shortcuts for gate-safe commands; registry includes /subagents (auth-gated, management category). connector-catalog.ts filters client commands off chat connectors. No coding-task-specific shortcut exists; coding tasks are surfaced via the planner action TASKS, not a dedicated slash command.

capacitor-eliza-tasks bridges iOS BGTaskScheduler/APNs wakes to a uniform 'wake' event; capacitor-mobile-signals bridges wake/lock/battery/health; plugin-native-reminders is a macOS Reminders bridge. These are generic infrastructure, not channels that surface coding-task progress.

gaps.title: No test asserts coding-task completion emits an AgentNotification
gaps.description: sub-agent-router.ts:831-854 fires getNotifier(runtime).notify({category:'agent', deepLink:'/orchestrator', groupKey:'orchestrator:<sessionId>'}) on task_complete, but sub-agent-router.test.ts has ZERO references to notify/Notifier (confirmed by grep). A regression that drops or mis-shapes the notification — the primary push-to-user/home-surface signal for finished coding tasks — would pass CI silently.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a unit test in sub-agent-router.test.ts that registers a mock ServiceType.NOTIFICATION service and asserts notify is called once on task_complete with title containing the label, category 'agent', source 'orchestrator', deepLink '/orchestrator', and groupKey 'orchestrator:<sessionId>'; assert it is NOT called for error/blocked/streaming events.

gaps.title: emitProgress routing ladder + cadence is untested
gaps.description: The thread→edit→send ladder, ACPX_PROGRESS_DELAY_MS debounce, ack-mode one-shot suppression, per-room ACK dedup, heartbeat 10s/30s loop, and the sendMessageToTarget thread-redirect wrapper are ~600 lines of intricate, regression-prone logic in index.ts with no direct tests (only the pure policy parser is covered). The 'don't be annoying' guarantees (no duplicate ACKs, silence over repetition, delay-then-post) are unverified.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a unit test that constructs registerProgressHook against a mock runtime exposing getMessageConnectors with controllable capabilities and spy send/edit/createThread/postToThread/addReaction. Drive synthetic AcpService events and assert: (a) compact mode posts one debounced fresh message after delayMs; (b) ack mode posts exactly one ACK per room within ACK_ROOM_DEDUP_MS and never edits; (c) silent mode posts nothing; (d) threaded mode creates one thread per label and routes narration there; (e) edit-capable surface edits one message in place; (f) heartbeat skips when no content and dedupes identical lines.

gaps.title: No end-to-end surfacing test through a real connector
gaps.description: There is no integration/e2e test that runs a coding task and asserts a progress message and a completion summary actually arrive on Discord and Telegram (the two connectors with differing capability sets — Discord edits/reacts, Telegram only threads/sends). The capability fork (edit_message present vs absent) is the highest-risk surface and is exercised only by isolated connector-registration tests.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a scenario-runner scenario (or connector live-smoke) that spawns a short sub-agent task and verifies, against Discord (full caps) and Telegram (no edit/react), that exactly one spawn ACK + one completion summary land, threads are created only where supported, and no duplicate/empty messages appear. Capture the trajectory + connector message log as evidence.

gaps.title: Zero trajectory/log/video/timeline evidence for the surfacing domain
gaps.description: Per AGENTS.md, agent/action/prompt changes require real-LLM trajectories, connector logs, before/after screenshots, and a video walkthrough. For coding-task surfacing none exist: no scenario trajectory, no captured progress timeline, no video of progress arriving in a chat or as a notification, no issue-evidence artifacts for this domain.
gaps.suggestedWork: Build a recorded walkthrough harness that runs a real coding task, captures (1) the structured [orchestrator] progress/heartbeat/notify logs, (2) a timeline of cadence events (spawn ACK at T0, heartbeat ticks, completion), (3) screenshots/video of the message in Discord/Telegram and the in-app/OS notification, and lands them under .github/issue-evidence/.
