No test confirms an orchestrator 'agent' notification with deepLink '/orchestrator' actually reaches a desktop OS or mobile local notification and deep-links correctly on tap.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add an integration test that ingests an orchestrator-shaped AgentNotification into the notification store while window unfocused and asserts the desktop+native sinks fire with the correct title/body/deepLink; add a mobile bridge test stubbing Capacitor.Plugins.LocalNotifications and asserting schedule() is called with the derived id and the /orchestrator deep link.

gaps.title: No coding-task slash command / shortcut / home-screen widget on connectors
gaps.description: Coding tasks are surfaced through the planner TASKS action and a chat [TASK:..] marker, plus auth-gated /subagents. There is no first-class slash command to start/check a coding task from a connector, no home-screen widget specifically for coding-task status (the native plugins are generic device bridges), and client/view commands are deliberately filtered off chat connectors. Users on Discord/Telegram cannot deterministically query coding-task status without the LLM routing.
gaps.suggestedWork: Decide whether to add a connector-safe agent-target command (e.g. /tasks or /coding) that lists active sub-agent sessions and progress via TASKS_LIST_AGENTS, register it as an explicit shortcut, and test it through connector-catalog (must appear on chat connectors, unlike client commands). If a mobile home-screen widget for coding-task status is intended, add it to plugin-native-* with a status feed; currently none exists.

gaps.title: ACK mode and ELIZA_SUB_AGENT_PROGRESS_* aliases are under-documented vs implemented
gaps.description: The code supports an 'ack' progress mode and ELIZA_SUB_AGENT_PROGRESS_MODE/_DELAY_MS/_REACTIONS aliases plus values off/disabled/none/0/false→silent, but the package CLAUDE.md documents only compact/threaded/silent for ACPX_PROGRESS_MODE. Operators tuning the 'don't be annoying' behavior may not discover ack mode (clean ack+final UX with no in-place edits).
gaps.suggestedWork: Update plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/CLAUDE.md (and AGENTS.md copy) to document the 'ack' mode, the ELIZA_SUB_AGENT_PROGRESS_* aliases, and the accepted silent synonyms, matching parseProgressMode in src/index.ts.

domain: L — Multiple simultaneous tasks/projects (concurrency, session/workspace isolation, scheduling, stress to 10)
summary: Concurrency for multiple simultaneous coding sub-agents lives almost entirely in plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator. There are two distinct limiters: a hard session cap ELIZA_ACP_MAX_SESSIONS (default 8) enforced atomically via a promise-chain mutex (reserveSessionSlot + enforceSessionLimit in acp-service.ts), and a soft spawn-gate ELIZA_MAX_CONCURRENT_SPAWNS (default 2, despite README/CLAUDE.md saying 'unset') implemented as a polling wait in actions/common.ts:waitForSpawnSlot. Session state is persisted through a tiered SessionStore (runtime-db / file-with-lock / in-memory) with a WriteQueue that serializes writes, plus startup orphan reconciliation, a 60s health-check sweep, retention GC, and reverse-orphan acpx file cleanup. The hard-cap atomicity is genuinely well-tested (a 6-spawn race against cap=2), but the system is NOT configured to handle 10 simultaneous projects out of the box: maxSessions defaults to 8 (<10) and the spawn-gate serializes to 2-at-a-time. The most serious correctness issue is that default workspace isolation does not exist — when a task matches no route/convention/explicit workdir, every concurrent session collapses to the SAME directory (process.cwd() or ELIZA_ACP_WORKSPACE_ROOT); git-worktree isolation is opt-in via useWorktree. There is no end-to-end/stress test (and no trajectory/log/screenshot/timeline evidence) for N independent simultaneous projects; SessionEventQueue is defined but completely unwired.

existing.component: AcpService session cap (ELIZA_ACP_MAX_SESSIONS, default 8) + atomic reserveSessionSlot/enforceSessionLimit mutex
existing.whatItDoes: Caps concurrent non-terminal sessions at maxSessions (default 8). reserveSessionSlot (lines ~2000-2014) is a promise-chain mutex that wraps enforceSessionLimit (list+count) and store.create into one critical section so N concurrent spawns cannot overshoot the cap. Also installs one process-wide SIGTERM/SIGINT shutdown hook via a static liveInstances registry, a 60s health-check, orphan reconciliation, and retention sweep.

Lines 343-379. Before each orchestrated spawn (called from actions/tasks.ts:876), polls real session state every 3s and blocks until active sessions < limit (default 2), giving up after 8min and proceeding anyway. Exists because gpt-oss-class models on OpenAI-compatible providers silently skip tool calls under concurrent load. Default is 2, contradicting the README/CLAUDE.md which list it as 'unset'.

AcpSessionStore selects runtime-db > file > memory. InMemorySessionStore serializes mutations through a WriteQueue promise chain; FileSessionStore adds an atomic temp+rename write under a stale-aware file lock (FILE_LOCK_ACQUIRE_TIMEOUT_MS=30s); RuntimeDbSessionStore uses INSERT OR REPLACE with its own WriteQueue. sweepStale removes terminal sessions past retention.

provisionWorkspace picks strategy 'worktree' vs 'clone' from options.useWorktree (line 439). Tracks workspaces in a Map, schedules per-session scratch cleanup timers (scheduleScratchCleanup), reads ELIZA_SCRATCH_RETENTION / ELIZA_SCRATCH_DECISION_TTL_MS, defaults workspaceTtlMs to 24h, and runs startup orphan-workspace GC. Worktree isolation is OPT-IN only.

existing.component: resolveSpawnWorkdir — default workspace resolution (the isolation gap)
existing.whatItDoes: Resolves spawn cwd via route > convention > explicit > default. resolveDefaultSpawnWorkdir (lines 142-156) falls back to a SINGLE shared dir: ELIZA_ACP_WORKSPACE_ROOT / ACPX_DEFAULT_CWD, or process.cwd() when unset. No per-session subdir is created, so multiple concurrent ad-hoc tasks with no route/worktree all write into the same directory.
removeScratchDir refuses to rm outside allowed base dirs; gcOrphanedWorkspaces removes untracked dirs older than workspaceTtlMs (TTL=0 disables). Keeps tracked workspace IDs safe.

Builds a Smithers workflow that runs `agents = max(1, parallelAgents)` agent loops; uses wf.parallel(...loops) when agents>1 (line 191). This is N agents on ONE task, NOT N independent projects. Durable resume across crash is supported.

Implements per-session FIFO queues with a processing Set so events for one session are delivered in order without head-of-line blocking across sessions. However grep shows ZERO importers in src — it is defined and exported but completely unwired; ordered delivery is not actually used by AcpService/SubAgentRouter.

existing.component: OrchestratorTaskService — concurrent sub-agents per task + distinct naming
existing.whatItDoes: Durable task store and lifecycle bridge from ACP events to task records; spawnAgentForTask assigns distinct pooled names so two concurrent sub-agents on one task don't collide.

existing.component: GET /api/orchestrator/rooms — workbench surfacing of concurrent sessions
existing.whatItDoes: Lines ~214-303. Returns a per-room participant roster grouping live sessions by room (orchestrator + user + sub-agents), omitting rooms with no sub-agents; emits widget refresh on status/usage changes so the workbench reflects multiple live sessions.

Bridges OS background wake-ups (Capacitor BGTaskScheduler/WorkManager) or a setInterval fallback to core TaskService.runDueTasks(); single periodic wake, not a concurrent sub-agent scheduler. Unrelated to ACP session concurrency despite being in scope; no overlap/concurrency handling for simultaneous wakes.

tests.covers: The headline concurrency test: 'enforces ELIZA_ACP_MAX_SESSIONS atomically under concurrent spawns' (line 1528) fires 6 simultaneous spawnSession calls with cap=2 via Promise.allSettled and asserts exactly 2 fulfilled, 4 rejected with 'max session limit reached', and store agrees only 2 active. Proves reserveSessionSlot is race-free. Also covers cancel SIGTERM/SIGKILL, orphan reconcile windows.
tests.gaps: Only tests the hard cap atomicity with the native fast path. Does NOT test cap at the documented default (8) or near 10. No test that the cap and the spawn-gate interact. No test of mid-flight session limit + retention sweep interplay under load.

tests.covers: 'serializes concurrent writes' for InMemorySessionStore (25 parallel creates) and FileSessionStore (20 parallel creates) both assert full count survives; stale-lock recovery; sweepStale selects only old stopped/errored; lastActivityAt transitions; findByScope.
tests.gaps: No cross-process file-lock contention test (two FileSessionStore instances racing on the same file). No RuntimeDbSessionStore concurrency test. No test that 10 sessions persist+reload correctly across a simulated restart.

tests.covers: 'assigns distinct names to two concurrent sub-agents on one task' (line 245) — two spawnAgentForTask on one task yield 2 sessions with distinct non-empty labels.
tests.gaps: Only 2 sub-agents, on a single task, via FakeAcp. No test of many tasks each with sub-agents concurrently; no real subprocess; no workspace isolation assertion.

tests.covers: 'fans out parallel agents and completes when all are done' (line 144) with parallelAgents:2 asserts 2 agents x 2 turns; plus durable crash-resume skipping completed turns.
tests.gaps: parallelAgents tested only at 2 and with a FakeExecutor (no real ACP turns). This is intra-task fan-out, not N independent projects. No stress beyond 2.

tests.covers: Explicit workdir fallback, ELIZA_ACP_WORKSPACE_ROOT / ACPX_DEFAULT_CWD precedence, convention auto-detect, self-checkout default to process.cwd(), malformed route guard.
tests.gaps: Crucially does NOT assert that two concurrent tasks get DISTINCT isolated workdirs — it actually documents/locks-in the shared-dir default. No test for collision when N sessions land on the same fallback dir.

tests.covers: GET /api/orchestrator/rooms groups live sessions by room with orchestrator+user+sub-agents, omits empty rooms, serves roster over the route.
tests.gaps: No assertion of correct grouping/ordering under many (e.g. 10) concurrent rooms/sessions; no large-roster or churn-under-load test.

tests.covers: One real acpx subprocess runs codex and emits task_complete routed back as a synthetic memory (single session).
tests.gaps: Single session only. No live multi-session/concurrent stress; gated on acpx being installed so usually skipped in CI.

tests.covers: Composed offline pipeline: AccountPool -> selector bridge -> AcpService.spawnSession -> real (fake) subprocess receiving injected per-account creds; proves multi-account selection end-to-end.
tests.gaps: Spawns are SEQUENTIAL (spawn 1 then spawn 2), not concurrent. Does not exercise the session cap, spawn-gate, or workspace isolation under simultaneous load.

tests.covers: BgTaskSchedulerService scheduler selection, serverless flag, wake->runDueTasks wiring; IntervalBgScheduler fallback.
tests.gaps: No overlapping-wake / re-entrancy / concurrent runDueTasks test.

evidence.where: No real-LLM trajectory exists for N simultaneous coding tasks. scenario-runner has no concurrency scenario referencing ELIZA_ACP_MAX_SESSIONS; the only multi-agent harness (smithers parallelAgents) is unit-tested with FakeExecutor.

Structured spawn-gate/health-check/reconcile logs exist in code ([spawn-gate], 'health-check self-healed sessions', 'reconcile: ...', 'coding account selected for spawn') but no captured log artifact of a concurrent N-session run is committed.
No screenshot of the orchestrator workbench/rooms view with multiple (let alone 10) concurrent sessions. .github/issue-evidence/ contains only unrelated PNGs (8810 banner, 8812 boot KPI).
No video walkthrough of running multiple simultaneous projects.
No timeline/Gantt capture of concurrent session lifecycles (spawn/queue/run/complete) exists.
packages/benchmarks/ORCHESTRATOR_SUBAGENT_BENCHMARK_RUNBOOK.md documents an orchestrator sub-agent benchmark but contains NO concurrency/N-simultaneous-tasks/stress section.

gaps.title: System cannot handle 10 simultaneous projects with default config
gaps.description: Two defaults block 10-way concurrency out of the box: ELIZA_ACP_MAX_SESSIONS defaults to 8 (acp-service.ts line 244), which is < 10, so the 9th/10th spawn rejects with 'max session limit reached'; and ELIZA_MAX_CONCURRENT_SPAWNS defaults to 2 (common.ts line 354), so the spawn-gate serializes orchestrated spawns to 2-at-a-time. Reaching 10 concurrent requires raising both, but there is no validated profile proving the runtime + model providers survive 10 concurrent sub-agents.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a documented 'high-concurrency' config profile (ELIZA_ACP_MAX_SESSIONS>=10, ELIZA_MAX_CONCURRENT_SPAWNS tuned) and a stress test that spawns 10 sessions and asserts all reach ready/complete. Reconcile the README/CLAUDE.md claim that ELIZA_MAX_CONCURRENT_SPAWNS is 'unset' with the actual default of 2.

gaps.title: No default per-session workspace isolation — concurrent ad-hoc tasks collide in one directory
gaps.description: resolveDefaultSpawnWorkdir (task-agent-routing.ts lines 142-156) collapses any task without a route/convention/explicit workdir to a SINGLE shared dir (ELIZA_ACP_WORKSPACE_ROOT/ACPX_DEFAULT_CWD or process.cwd()). Git-worktree isolation is opt-in (useWorktree). Running multiple simultaneous projects without explicit routing means all sub-agents read/write the same files, corrupting each other's work — exactly the failure mode for '10 simultaneous projects'. resolve-spawn-workdir.test.ts locks in this shared-dir behavior rather than guarding against it.
gaps.suggestedWork: Default to a per-session isolated subdir (e.g. <root>/<sessionId>) when no explicit workdir/route is given, OR auto-enable a worktree per concurrent session. Add a test asserting two concurrent spawns with no route get distinct workdirs, and a stress test asserting no cross-session file writes.

gaps.title: No end-to-end / stress test for N simultaneous coding tasks
gaps.description: The only concurrency test is the unit-level cap-atomicity test (6 spawns, cap 2, native fast path). There is no test that spins up N (e.g. 8-10) real or realistically-faked sub-agent sessions across distinct tasks, drives them through provision->turn->complete, and asserts isolation, correct workbench/rooms surfacing, retention sweep, and no slot leak. The multi-account e2e spawns sequentially.
gaps.suggestedWork: Author a scenario-runner scenario (and a composed offline harness like compose-multi-account-e2e but concurrent) that launches 10 tasks via TASKS_CREATE, captures a real-LLM trajectory per session, asserts all complete in isolated workspaces, and emits a timeline. Lower ACPX_SUB_AGENT_ROUND_TRIP_CAP for the test env.

gaps.title: SessionEventQueue is dead/unwired code
gaps.description: session-event-queue.ts implements per-session ordered, non-head-of-line-blocking event delivery, but grep shows zero importers in src. AcpService/SubAgentRouter route events without it, so under concurrent load there is no guarantee that blocked/turn_complete events for a session are delivered in order, and a slow handler for one session can block others.
gaps.suggestedWork: Either wire SessionEventQueue into SubAgentRouter/AcpService event emission (and test ordered delivery + cross-session isolation under interleaved events) or delete it as dead code.

gaps.title: No cross-process / multi-instance file-lock contention test
gaps.description: FileSessionStore uses an O_EXCL lock + stale-lock recovery to coordinate writes, but tests only cover single-instance in-process serialization. Two AcpService instances (multi-tenant, hot-reload overlap) hitting the same sessions.json concurrently is untested, and a lost-write or stale-lock thrash there would silently drop sessions.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a test that creates two FileSessionStore instances on the same file and races creates/updates, asserting no lost writes and correct stale-lock takeover.

gaps.title: Workbench surfacing of many concurrent sessions is unverified
gaps.description: GET /api/orchestrator/rooms grouping is tested only with a handful of sessions; there is no test or screenshot proving the rooms roster + widget refresh remain correct and performant with 8-10 live sessions churning status/usage. No frontend (packages/app) source was found wiring ELIZA_ACP_MAX_SESSIONS or a multi-session board.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add a route test with 10 concurrent rooms/sessions asserting correct grouping/ordering, and capture before/after screenshots of the workbench with 10 live sessions for evidence.

gaps.title: Spawn-gate (waitForSpawnSlot) has no unit test
gaps.description: waitForSpawnSlot (common.ts 343-379) is the primary throttle that prevents concurrent-load model degradation, but it has no test for: limit<=0 disabling the gate, the 8min maxWaitMs giveup-and-proceed path, or correct active-session counting that excludes terminal statuses.
gaps.suggestedWork: Add unit tests for waitForSpawnSlot covering disabled (limit 0/negative), blocking-then-releasing as sessions terminate, and the giveup-after-maxWaitMs warn-and-proceed path.
